skill.name: bazel-build-optimization
skill.description: Optimize Bazel builds for large-scale monorepos. Use when configuring Bazel, implementing remote execution, or optimizing build performance for enterprise codebases.
skill.summary: Optimize Bazel builds for large-scale monorepos with remote caching, custom rules, and performance profiling.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 36 potential issues (external_commands, network, filesystem patterns), but all are false positives. The skill contains documentation and templates for Bazel build optimization with example commands and configuration snippets - no actual executable code with security implications. URLs are legitimate documentation links, filesystem references are standard Bazel cache paths, and cryptographic mentions are SHA256 checksums for download verification.

content.value_statement: Bazel builds in large monorepos can be slow and complex. This skill provides proven patterns for remote caching, parallel execution, and custom rules to dramatically reduce build times.
actual_capabilities: Configure Bazel WORKSPACE files with external dependencies
Set up remote caching and execution for distributed teams
Write custom Bazel rules for specialized build tasks
Optimize build performance with profiling and analysis
Create efficient BUILD files with fine-grained targets
Debug build issues using Bazel query and analysis tools
limitations: Does not install Bazel itself - users need Bazel pre-installed
Cannot modify existing project BUILD files without user approval
Does not provide runtime execution of build commands
Focuses on Bazel - not applicable to other build systems

use_cases.title: Set up remote caching for CI/CD pipelines
use_cases.description: Configure Bazel with remote caching to share build artifacts across CI runners and reduce build times from hours to minutes.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers and build infrastructure teams

use_cases.title: Optimize a large TypeScript monorepo
use_cases.description: Apply best practices for organizing BUILD files, enabling parallel execution, and configuring disk caching.

use_cases.title: Create custom build rules for Docker
use_cases.description: Write a custom Bazel rule to build Docker images as part of the build graph with proper dependency tracking.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Bazel setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Bazel for a new monorepo. I need to configure WORKSPACE.bazel with rules for JavaScript and Python. What are the essential configurations?
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with Bazel in a new project

prompt_templates.title: Enable remote caching
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to set up remote caching for our Bazel builds. We use Google Cloud. Show me the .bazelrc configuration needed and any security considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Write a custom rule
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a custom Bazel rule that builds a Docker image. The rule should accept a Dockerfile, base image, and layer files as inputs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Extending Bazel with custom build logic

prompt_templates.title: Profile and optimize build
prompt_templates.prompt: Our Bazel build takes 30 minutes. Help me profile it using bazel build --profile and identify the slowest actions. What optimizations should I try first?

output_examples.input: Help me set up Bazel for a Python project with external dependencies
output_examples.output:
Here is a basic WORKSPACE.bazel configuration for Python:

```python
workspace(name = "myproject")

load("@bazel_tools//tools/build_defs/repo:http.bzl", "http_archive")

http_archive(
    name = "rules_python",
    sha256 = "...",
    strip_prefix = "rules_python-0.27.0",
    url = "https://github.com/bazelbuild/rules_python/releases/download/0.27.0/rules_python-0.27.0.tar.gz",
)

load("@rules_python//python:repositories.bzl", "py_repositories")
py_repositories()
```

Then create a BUILD.bazel file with py_library and py_test rules.

output_examples.input: How do I configure remote execution?
output_examples.output:
Add this to your .bazelrc for remote execution:

```bash
build:remote-exec --remote_executor=grpcs://remote.example.com
build:remote-exec --remote_instance_name=projects/myproject/instances/default
build:remote-exec --jobs=500
```

Security tips:
- Use TLS for remote endpoints
- Authenticate with OAuth or certificates
- Start with read-only remote cache before enabling execution

best_practices: Use fine-grained targets instead of large glob patterns for better incremental builds
Enable remote caching early - it provides the biggest performance gains
Pin dependency versions in WORKSPACE to ensure reproducible builds
anti_patterns: Using glob(['**/*.py']) for srcs - causes unnecessary rebuilds
Skipping WORKSPACE setup - leads to inconsistent dependency resolution
Ignoring build warnings - small issues become technical debt

faq.question: Does this skill install Bazel?
faq.answer: No, Bazel must be pre-installed on your system. This skill helps you configure and optimize Bazel once it is installed.

faq.question: Can I use this for non-Bazel build systems?
faq.answer: No, this skill is specifically for Bazel. It covers WORKSPACE, BUILD files, .bazelrc, and Bazel-specific concepts.

faq.question: How much build time can I save?
faq.answer: With remote caching, teams typically see 50-90% reduction in build times after the first build. Remote execution can provide even greater improvements for large codebases.

faq.question: Is remote caching secure?
faq.answer: Yes, when properly configured with TLS and authentication. Start with a read-only cache to verify security before enabling write access.

faq.question: Can this help with migration to Bazel?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes guidance on migrating to Bazel from other build systems and setting up initial WORKSPACE configurations.

faq.question: What languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill covers JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, Java, C++, and Docker. It shows how to configure rules for each language ecosystem.
